## Meeting Purpose

[Interviewing Leonardo Steffen for the Chief of Staff, CTO role.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Role Context:** The CTO needs a "force multiplier" to manage a 15–20 person Eng org and 4–5 simultaneous product lanes, requiring a partner for context-switching and QA.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
  - [**Problem-Solving:** At GuestLogix, Steffen cut scope on a Wi-Fi library project, prioritizing a working app for a critical deadline over a reusable library that was causing delays.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=821.0)
  - [**Process Navigation:** At Ryan Tax, Steffen navigated a bureaucratic org by escalating review requests to a director, leveraging their authority to unblock a critical multi-tenancy merge.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
  - [**Candidate Fit:** Steffen's "generalizing specialist" profile and efficiency-first mindset align with the role's need for a flexible, high-leverage partner.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2374.0)

## Topics

### The CTO's Need for a "Force Multiplier"

  - [The CTO (Daniel) requires a Chief of Staff to manage a 15–20 person Eng org and 4–5 simultaneous product lanes.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)
  - [The role is a "force multiplier" to provide context-switching support and QA oversight, enabling the CTO to focus on high-leverage work.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=429.0)

### Problem-Solving: Prioritizing User Value over Engineering Purity

  - [**Context:** At GuestLogix, the Android app needed an offline Wi-Fi service for in-flight use.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=833.0)
  - [**Dilemma:** Build the service as a reusable library (per a tech lead's request) or integrate it directly into the app.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=878.0)
  - [**Decision:** After one month of work, the library approach proved too complex and risked missing the deadline. Steffen advocated for the direct integration.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=821.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Prioritized user value (a working app) over engineering purity (reusability), using the deadline and risk of a non-functional product as the primary arguments.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)

### Navigating Bureaucracy: Unblocking a Critical Merge

  - [**Context:** At Ryan Tax, a critical multi-tenancy merge was blocked by delayed code reviews, stalling other teams. The deadline was today.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1565.0)
  - [**Challenge:** The org's bureaucracy and a contractor status limited Steffen's direct influence over reviewers.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2224.0)
  - [**Action:**](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1657.0)
      - [Categorized review feedback into critical, good-to-have, and cosmetic.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1660.0)
      - [Escalated the issue to the director, who used their authority to push for reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2224.0)
      - [Secured agreement from reviewers to approve the merge after only the critical changes were made, unblocking the project.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1684.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** The project was broken down horizontally (by controller) instead of vertically (by full-stack feature), creating interdependencies and a single large branch that was difficult to review.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=1950.0)

### Work Style & Feedback

  - [**Energizers:** Solving new problems, learning, and operating as a "generalizing specialist."](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2374.0)
  - [**Drainers:** Process overhead (e.g., 5-layer story tracking) and unproductive debates (e.g., Vim vs. Emacs).](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2444.0)
  - [**Constructive Feedback:** A colleague suggested sharing detailed written plans in stand-ups to provide more context.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2656.0)
  - [**Positive Feedback:** Praised for adaptability and humility, especially in admitting when knowledge is lacking.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=2902.0)
  - [**Self-Improvement:** Learning to say "no" to manage workload and avoid overcommitment.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=3054.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Bridget McGillivray:** Check in with GP and Tucker regarding the interview.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=3678.0)
  - [**Tucker:** Contact Steffen with next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/ssz8sN76Jtpz2FHj7YgRUHGvawszSt1r?tab=summary&timestamp=3687.0)
